content.full_text:
FPPC
SEP 2 0 1991

OFFICE OF THE
CITY ATTORNEY

CITY OF SACRAMENTO
CALIFORNIA

921 TENTH STREET
SUITE 700
SACRAMENTO, CA
95814-2717

SHARON SIEIDOR CARDENAS
CITY ATTORNEY

PH. 916-449-5346
FAX 916-449-6755

THEODORE H. KOBEY, JR.
ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY

DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEYS:
EVELYN M. MATTEUCGI
DIANE B. BALTER
RICHARD F. ANTOINE
TAMARA MILLIGAN HARMON
RICHARD E. ARCHIBALD
TIMOTHY N. WASHBURN
SABRINA M. THOMPSON
JOSEPH McINERNEY
JOE ROBINSON
LESLIE R. LOPEZ

SR. DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEYS:
SAMUEL L. JACKSON
WILLIAM P. CARNAZZO

Bianca Breeze, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Conflict of Interest -- Calling of Special Meetings

Dear Ms. Breeze:

     This will confirm our conversation this date, wherein we discussed whether a conflict
of interest on the part of the presiding member of a legislative body prevented that official
from calling a special meeting pursuant to the Brown Act (Government Code §54956) at
which the item on which the official had a conflict would be discussed. You advised that
the mere calling of the meeting was not an act prohibited by §87100 of the Government
Code, and it was therefore permissible. It is agreed and understood that the elected official
will abstain from any participation or involvement in the item as to which an actual or
potential conflict exists.

     Thank you for your advice in this matter. You graciously offered to confirm this
advice in a follow-up letter, and I would respectfully request that you do so.

                                                Very truly yours,

                                                [signature]

                                                RICHARD E. ARCHIBALD,
                                                Deputy City Attorney

REA/jms

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a presiding member of a legislative body with a conflict of interest may call a special meeting under the Brown Act to discuss an item on which they have a conflict of interest.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised (in a prior telephone conversation) that the mere calling of the meeting is not an act prohibited by Government Code §87100, and is therefore permissible, provided the official abstains from any participation or involvement in the conflicted item.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence letter from a city attorney requesting written confirmation of prior FPPC telephone advice. No formal question/conclusion sections present. The substantive legal advice was provided orally; this letter documents and requests written confirmation. The FPPC response (if any) would be separate.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a presiding member of a legislative body with a conflict of interest may call a special meeting under the Brown Act to discuss an item on which they have a conflict of interest.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised (in a prior telephone conversation) that the mere calling of the meeting is not an act prohibited by Government Code §87100, and is therefore permissible, provided the official abstains from any participation or involvement in the conflicted item.

You advised that the mere calling of the meeting was not an act prohibited by §87100 of the Government Code, and it was therefore permissible. It is agreed and understood that the elected official will abstain from any participation or involvement in the item as to which an actual or potential conflict exists. embedding.summary: A City of Sacramento Deputy City Attorney writes to the FPPC to confirm a prior telephone conversation with an FPPC attorney regarding whether a legislative body member with a conflict of interest may call a special meeting to discuss the conflicted item under the Brown Act.
